item.title: Britain’s questionable reliance on Palantir
item.description: Government should use the best technology for any task, but be wary of ‘lock-ins’

item.title: British Airways chief says air fares will rise again if fuel costs stay high
item.description: Sean Doyle’s warning comes as jet fuel prices have doubled since Iran war began in February

item.title: OpenAI plots biggest ChatGPT overhaul since launch
item.description: $850bn start-up to recast hit chatbot as a route to higher-margin products before a potential IPO

item.title: Expensive oil is making electric vehicles look positively cheap
item.description: Petrol retails for £1.58 a litre in the UK, up about 20% since the start of the year

item.title: Europe risks ‘mass unemployment’ without reform, warns ABB boss
item.description: Morten Wierod calls for urgent deregulation as energy shock from Iran war dents EU competitiveness

item.title: We are optimising ourselves to death
item.description: A tracker might tell you that you got to bed too late, but it doesn’t tell you that you were busy living your life

item.title: Italian defence and engineering groups enjoy Gulf deal boost
item.description: Italy has proved it is a ‘reliable partner at the darkest of times’, say analysts

item.title: Longest-serving CEO in Fortune 250: ‘You have to know your limits’
item.description: Dan Amos and his quacking duck advert have made insurer Aflac a household name in the US

item.title: Ferrari diehards debate whether buying its first EV is test of loyalty
item.description: Group has banned dealers from bundling Luce sales and access to exclusive models as it seeks new clients

item.title: Nato narrowly beats Russia-style enemy in cyber attack simulation
item.description: Alliance tests government response to online propaganda mirroring Moscow’s wartime tactics

item.title: We should be getting better at AI by now
item.description: From cancelled novels to legal fines, the scale of blunders only halfway through the year suggests the opposite

item.title: Tokio Marine boss to use Berkshire deal for global growth drive
item.description: Masahiro Koike aims to turn Japanese group into top five global insurer within a decade

item.title: UK nuclear weapons spending not transparent enough says watchdog
item.description: Public Accounts Committee also criticises lengthy delay to government’s defence investment plan

item.title: Bouygues Telecom consortium agrees to buy Patrick Drahi’s SFR for €20.35bn
item.description: Bid from group including Orange and Free-Iliad faces showdown with antitrust regulators in Paris and Brussels

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s Etihad to surpass pre-Iran War capacity without price cuts
item.description: Airline had its planes grounded when fighting began but demand for transit through region has recovered

item.title: What we know about the plan to give Americans an equity stake in AI
item.description: OpenAI has proposed a sovereign-wealth-style fund to ease public anxiety about the impact of artificial intelligence

item.title: West Ham co-chair quits ahead of ‘publication of serious historic allegations’
item.description: David Sullivan departs just two weeks after club was relegated from Premier League

item.title: Oil tanker owners fear market crash after Iran war drove record profits
item.description: Shipowners ploughed windfall into new vessels and are braced for steep drop in rates if Strait of Hormuz reopens

item.title: One billion pints: beer giants prepare for booziest World Cup in history
item.description: An expanded tournament held in beer-friendly countries will result in an extra 1bn pints being consumed, say analysts

item.title: How Monaco changed for F1
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: The $10bn club, pickleball’s rise and more

item.title: Peru to vote on ninth president in a decade
item.description: Neither candidate, who offer widely differing agendas, obtained 20% in first round of voting

item.title: Can AI save a company’s soul?
item.description: It is easy to imagine institutional memory lost to automation preserved in silicon — but there is one crucial difference

item.title: AstraZeneca chief warns group could withhold new drugs in Europe
item.description: Sir Pascal Soriot says countries will have to spend more on innovative medicines following US trade deal

item.title: Investors wake up to the merits of sleep biotech
item.description: More people worry about poor sleep than excess weight, but drugmakers have been deterred from entering the market by costs

item.title: On yer bike! Freud and middle-aged men in Lycra
item.description: The ever-present fear of ridicule among cycling’s weekend warriors tells us a thing or two about human nature

item.title: Irish government under pressure over alumina exports to Russia
item.description: Lawmakers call for EU sanctions on Oleg Deripaska-linked plant in Ireland if raw material is used in Russian weapons

item.title: In World Cup season, choose your financial trophies carefully
item.description: Real Madrid’s president has made it very clear that investing in the club is mainly about having fun and making contacts

item.title: Wasting China’s solar panel surplus is madness
item.description: Clean power is within our reach — yet factories sit idle

item.title: Directors’ Deals: BP veteran hives off nearly £2mn in shares ahead of Manifold brouhaha
item.description: The group has been struggling to convince the market that it now possesses greater strategic clarity

item.title: How inheritance tax prompted the great pensions rethink
item.description: With the rules changing next year, savers need to reconsider what they do with their ‘red money’
